title: Daily Briefing — Apr 03
headline: Sentiment's in the toilet, but bonds are stable and energy's sneaking higher — contrarian setups incoming.
body: Right, big picture: Market stuck in chop with SPY ($655.83) and QQQ ($584.98) wrestling the 20 MA from below. VIX remains elevated at 23.87 but is now trending under its SMA20, so volatility's cooling off. Fear & Greed has collapsed to 19.3 (Extreme Fear) — last time sentiment looked this bleak, we got a mean reversion rally within days.

Rotation: Energy (XLE $59.25) is still strong, well above its 20 MA, but momentum is waning. Tech is mostly underwater (XLK $135.99, 20 MA at $136.18), but improvement in momentum hints at possible snapbacks for leaders like AAPL ($255.92) and ARM ($149.11). Healthcare and Consumer Discretionary are lagging; Utilities just popping but likely short-lived with weakening momentum.

Contrarian/risk: When Fear & Greed < 25 and VIX is high, patience pays. No decisive breakdowns in HYG/LQD—bonds playing nice (for now). If the press starts doom-casting, remember: "If it's in the press, it's in the price." Eyes on dark pool flows and weekly closes — don't chase weakness, this is abundance mindset territory. Record extreme fear is a strong signal to prep for mean reversion, not panic selling.
callout_body:
SPY: $658.09 (SMA20 overhead)
QQQ: $589.01 (SMA20 overhead)
XLE: $58.83 (SMA20 support)
XLK: $136.18 (SMA20 pivot)
AAPL: $253.41 (SMA20)
NVDA: $177.62 (SMA20 resistance)
Bonds: HYG $79.08, LQD $108.44 (SMA20 levels)
Fear & Greed: 19.3 — Extreme Fear (rule trigger)
Watch for weekly closes above 20 MA for entry signals
action_items: Wait for SPY/QQQ to close above their 20 MA — no long index exposure until confirmation. Patience, react, don't predict.
Monitor Energy and Tech leaders for pullback setups (XLE, ARM, AAPL). Extreme fear is when you prepare, not chase.
closing_quote: Extreme fear? That's opportunity, not a warning. Patience — the mean reversion zone is coming.
